title: A nonlinear Landau-Zener formula
comment:
33 pages. Major revision in order to fix a flaw in the case F_1 !=
  F_2 (the case F_1=F_2 is substantially simplified)
summary:
We consider a system of two coupled ordinary differential equations which
appears as an envelope equation in Bose-Einstein Condensation. This system can
be viewed as a nonlinear extension of the celebrated model introduced by Landau
and Zener. We show how the nonlinear system may appear from different physical
models. We focus our attention on the large time behavior of the solution. We
show the existence of a nonlinear scattering operator, which is reminiscent of
long range scattering for the nonlinear Schrodinger equation, and which can be
compared with its linear counterpart.

title: The Polar Cone of the set of monotone maps
summary:
We prove that every element of the polar cone to the closed convex cone of
monotone transport maps can be represented as the divergence of a measure field
taking values in the positive definite matrices.

title: Existence of Smooth Solutions of the Navier-Stokes Equations
summary:
In this paper, we prove existence of smooth solutions of the Navier-Stokes
equations that gives a positive answer to the problem proposed by Fefferman
[3].

title: On the rigidity of nematic liquid crystal flow on $S^2$
summary:
In this paper we establish the uniformity property of a simplified
Ericksen-Leslie system modelling the hydrodynamics of nematic liquid crystals
on the two dimensional unit sphere $S^2$, namely the uniform convergence in
$L^2$ to a steady state exponentially as t tends to infinity. The main
assumption, similar to Topping [15], concerns the equation of liquid crystal
director $d$ and states that at infinity time, a weak limit $d_\infty$ and any
bubble $\omega_i$($1\le i\le l$) share a common orientation. As consequences,
the uniformity property holds under various types of small initial data.

title: $L^{p}$ estimates for multi-linear and multi-parameter   pseudo-differential operators
summary:
We establish the pseudo-differential variant of the $L^{p}$ estimates for
multi-linear and multi-parameter Coifman-Meyer multiplier operators proved by
C. Muscalu, J. Pipher, T. Tao and C. Thiele in \cite{MPTT1,MPTT2}.

title: A symbolic calculus for Fourier integral operators
comment: To appear in the CRM conference proceeding series
summary:
The paper develops a symbolic calculus for Fourier integral operators
associated with canonical transformations.

title: The Compressible to Incompressible Limit of 1D Euler Equations: the Non   Smooth Case
summary:
We prove a rigorous convergence result for the compressible to incompressible
limit of weak entropy solutions to the isothermal 1D Euler equations.
